methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $280M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Chai Discovery
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series B), post-money $3.8B

private.name: Eikon Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series B), post-money $539M

data.narrative:
Genesis Therapeutics, a Series B-stage biotech company that has raised $280M+, is evaluated against a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector. The private comparables include Chai Discovery ($3.8B), Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B), New Limit ($3.1B), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M), spanning Series B and Series C stages.

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $280M raised proxy, implying a $532M valuation. The peer-median method anchors to the $3.5B median across the four identified private comparables, which range from $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not disclosed, a typical limitation for private-stage companies. The sector median P/S across 3 public biotech tickers is 6.9x, but remains unusable without revenue data.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $452M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $2.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material stage and scale heterogeneity within the peer set. Key uncertainties include the timing and probability of next-round funding, clinical or program-level inflection points, and capital efficiency relative to comparables at similar inflection points. The median of $2.0B sits materially above the stage-progression floor, suggesting peer-driven valuation dynamics dominate typical early-biotech step-up mechanics.
